terms.definition: Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection—a non-linear dimensionality-reduction method that matches fuzzy high-dimensional k-NN structure to a low-dimensional layout. Canonical treatment in this topic.

UMAP hyperparameter setting the size of each point's local neighborhood when building the high-dimensional k-NN graph; smaller values emphasize local structure, larger values emphasize global structure.
UMAP hyperparameter controlling minimum spacing between embedded points in low-dimensional space; lower values yield tighter clusters, higher values spread points for a more global layout.

Trade-off in non-linear visualization: local structure keeps true neighbors close in the plot; global structure preserves broader arrangement between distant regions. UMAP balances both via its fuzzy topological objective and hyperparameters.
Soft-weighted graph representation UMAP builds from k-NN connectivity—membership strengths encode how strongly points belong to local neighborhoods before embedding optimization.
Family of methods assuming data lie on a lower-dimensional curved surface embedded in high-dimensional space; UMAP is a manifold-learning embedder for visualization and non-linear DR.
